Hey, Phil.
Hey Iman.

Ok, give me a second
there.

Let me know when you're all set.
All right.
Yeah.
How are you guys today?
Not too bad.
Not too bad, busy Tuesday so far.
How about yourself?
Uh busy.
We're going into budgets tomorrow.
So like a lot of scrambling and running around right now.
Yeah.
Best time of the year, right?
Oh, exactly.
And we're doing a lunch order.
So.
Oh, goodness.
Well, thank you so much for making a little bit of time for me.
It sounds like you've got a lot going on.
Um But it's wonderful to meet you.
I um am I pronouncing that right?
By the way, I, I in excuse me.
Lovely name.
Um We have today as well, Zach from our product team.
He knows a whole lot about what's coming out with apartment IQ.
So just to kind of give you an idea of my agenda for today, I was hoping to learn a little bit about how your team has been using apartment IQ.
I see that you're the heavy user.
So I thought you might be a great resource.
Uh Talk a little bit about some of the things that are changing for apartment IQ, uh some additional features that are coming in and available.

And then Zach here will be able to highlight some exciting changes happening on those executive market surveys that I know you use a lot because that's in the pipe and no one's no one's seeing that yet.

So I thought it'd be fun to bring him in because you use it and get your feedback and then we'll kind of talk about uh 2024 ideally, um because I'm hoping that you guys will keep with us uh for the next year.
That sounds like an agenda.
That sounds like an agenda.
All right.
So then first, I'd love to open the floor to you.
Tell me a little bit about your role and, and how you've been finding apartment IQ most helpful.
How does it fit into your workflow?
Ok.
Um Well, I'm the sales and marketing director, so I oversee the leasing department.
Um I, I really use it for the market survey portion.
Like I'm always like, this one is always up and I'm just refreshing it and um alleviates the need to call every single property on the list and then be and then, you know, when we identify possible.
Um comps is very helpful when they, when we have access to their information right away too.
So I'm, I'm looking at pricing because it's a, it's a, it's a discussion.
We don't have a lot of price changes.
We're not in any pricing system.
So it's really, we come to the table when there's a need and then decide on price changes.
So I know that there's a lot of suggestions, but a lot of those properties are on pricing models.
So that fluctuates all the time.
But we don't so much.
Yeah, we run the same way but it is, it is helpful when I have to have discussions with the VP and the general manager about what we need to do for pricing.
So got it.
And so we're not chasing pricing all the time.
And I know that can be subjective from group to group because like you said, some people change it multiple times a day because they're on that pool.
Um How often are you meeting with your VPs and talking about pricing?
Is it several times a month?
One a month?
It's, it's usually once a month that we talk about prices.
Yeah.
And it's just, it's just to see like if we need to make any adjustments, um you know, for under market, especially with our, what we call classic units.
And so we have, we do have those discussions on a monthly basis, but I mean, when I say prices are usually just that we build it into our budget like a yearly price increase.
That's how much we're prices.
Ok.
Wow.
Yeah, that's a little bit different than what I hear sometimes.
So that is very interesting.
Talk to me about your properties.
Are they, you're not in student housing at all?
Right.
No, we're not student housing.
So it's actually just one property and it's 2877 units.
And so it operates like several different properties.
Um, we do, we, it's just straight market.
Um, we don't have student, any other type of, you know, nothing identifies specifically for students or anything like that.
Um But, you know, has the tide changes around here.
We are trying to look at different, um you know, places to look for new residents.
It's, it's, it's a very competitive market in the area.
Um We're right outside of college park and they have like literally, I mean, 20 it feels like 20 new buildings going on.
I'm pretty sure that's not how many there are, but they're constantly building over there.
So it's really trying to, you know, we understand that that's probably no longer gonna be our demographic.
So trying to step outside and identify other places that might need, um, have a need for housing, right?
And how do you guys do you manage?
I suppose benchmarks in your marketing team, like how do you evaluate the success of your marketing?
Is it advertised occupancy?
Is it leasing percentage?
What are some of the benchmarks you use?
It's, it's the is definitely occupancy hands down.

Um It's, you know where it's, so that's when we, you know, so then we start looking through our primary advertising sources and really identifying if we're getting the uh adequate number of leads for what we're paying for, you know, what's just spamming us.
Um, on a permit list is a big one.
But they also because we pay per lead, it isn't, it's worth keeping around.
Um, but it's definitely, it's definitely occupancy is the biggest, like occupancy is like everyday conversation here.
I see.
OK.
That's good to know because we are and, and Zach might be able to talk a little bit about this more, but we are reconfiguring how we're going to be capturing occupancy.
Um So that's something that's coming and should hopefully improve your experience going forward.
Um And I'm expecting by Christmas or by Q one.
We'll be, we'll be talking about actually using that new tool.
OK.
Cool.
Um But that sounds really great.
Thank you so much for kind of giving me that background.
I man.
Oh my God.
I'm so sorry, I'm in.
What am I doing?
I'm actually going to type it out like phonetically because I am terrible with names.
That is such a cool name.
Uh OK.
So as far as transitioning now, I feel like it's a good point to talk about the actual platform.
Um I'll go ahead and share my screen real quick uh because we have a couple of things to talk about some things that we've shipped here in Q three.
Um particularly things that I think might help you out.
Uh especially with such a competitive marketing landscape.
We have the ability now to pull in concessions and concessions details and then because we can grab that value, we can deliver an N er that effective rent on a unit by unit level.
We also have the ability to push out all of our unit information into a data warehouse.
So that if you guys are using any more complicated modeling on the back end, perhaps you find the finance side of the house.
Um We can push all the unit data that we have in apartment IQ directly into your models.
So your excels your power bis, your, your Google drives and, and snowflakes, that sort of thing.
And now rather than show you just a slide of concessions and a slide of N er I've gone ahead and turned it on in your account.
So popping in here, you'll notice three new columns here.
We have the concession details
which you can mouse over.
If there are multiple concessions, it'll pull the both of them and you can highlight exactly what's going on there.
And then we have the average concession if it's a value associated with a rent total and the average rent and average N er ok.
So those I think you, you mentioned that marketing can be tough.
I don't know how, how essential are concessions to your market?
Is it helpful to know that concessions are happening or is that something, you know, you, you solve for elsewhere?
It's definitely, um, helpful because we, um, it lets us know if we need to run concessions or not.
If everyone, you know, we need to, we need to be competitive with everyone else.
So it's, it's, it's important to know that if they're doing that or not.
Absolutely.
Sure.
Great to hear.
Um, and then a note on our advertised occupancy, this is more of a quality of life improvement.
But we've added these tags to any units that apartment IQ has identified as most likely to be in a lease up or they're maybe doing a phased out release of apartment units.
Um This way it doesn't mud this value too much as you've probably already noticed.
The advertised occupancy is based on how many units a landlord is showing on their website that given day, right?
So, could be different.
Of course, if maybe they're always highlighting a couple units and keep the real ones, you know, in the P MS system, right?

So, um, what we do think this can be valuable for is just to have a uniform way to measure this because I think occupancy can vary from person to person as we're talking about what we consider an advertised occupancy unit.
So, um, that is the big change there.
And now I'd like to maybe hand it over to Zach because this is the most exciting thing in my book right now, especially for a user like you who is really taking advantage of those PDF market surveys.
Uh Zach, I'm gonna go ahead and make you the host that you can share your screen.
Perfect, thanks.
Uh Yeah.
So I'm really curious.
Um Phil mentioned that you use the PDF reports.
Um And I'm curious how they've been helpful for you so far.
Um You know, I, well, obviously, obviously sharing the information with everyone.

I have a leasing manager that I um work with and just making sure she has the awareness of everything that's going on in the market too, so she can make any suggestions um since she is kind of on the front line at this point.
So,
ok, that makes sense.
Yeah.
So they're kind of using them to just look for changes to like make sure everyone's on the same page.
Yes.
Um How, how often is that happening?
Are you guys using those on weekly basis?
Well, I, so I, I honestly keep uh apartment IQ up on my desktop like every day, it's just up, it's, it's here.
I've just switched over to the start.
It's, it's literally just a refreshing.
So that way it is always readily available.
No.
Ok, great.
I'm gonna pull up my screen and share some new concepts that we have for these PDF reports and I'd love to get your thoughts on the, the concept that we're working through.
So like bill was saying earlier, um we've added a lot uh to apartment IQ in terms of concessions and net effective rent.
We wanted to reflect that in these PDF reports as well.
So as you can see, we've added a couple of columns for the concession amount, net effective rent and then net effective rent per square foot.
Um Another new thing that we're introducing, um we're looking at introducing is showing you trends.
So mentioned, you like to see what's changing in your market.
Um So hopefully something like this would make that a lot easier.
So you can see how these key metrics are changing over time.
Um So we would be kind of following that same pattern on the unit types stages as well.
So again, kind of surfacing those changes for specific um unit types and then again, bring in concessions that affect the brand and uh that's quite different for square but,
and then like I was saying, we're also, you know, have done a lot of good work on concessions.
We wanted to surface that here as well um with its own dedicated page.
So you can see which properties are offering concessions, what the details are around them and then which properties aren't offering any?
Ok, cool.
It's better than going to every single website every day.
Yes.
Yeah, absolutely.
Do you feel like these, how would these changes kind of um I guess, yeah, I'll just open the floor to you.
Like, what do you think about?
Well, I think definitely the concessions are adding that on as part of the reporting is immensely.
I, I, when I save our comps websites on my dashboard on my, you know, desktop, you know, in the thing, so I can check to see what they're doing.
Right.
Because sometimes you can't get a hold of someone to do a market survey.
Um, so it's, that would be great to have it all in one central location.
So I wouldn't have to keep, you know, it, it doesn't seem like it's time consuming with everything else going on.
It can definitely be easy.
It's much easier to have it all streamlined in one place
only.

And then I do like to highlight the another big change here on the unit type analysis is that we're getting rid of that static trend graph and instead replacing it with these, these KPIS for average rent per square foot, including a trend there.
How do you feel about that?
I mean, is that better for you?
You think or did you like that trend graph?
I'm just kind of, it's for, I think, you know, having it graphs are OK.
But having it just kind of out there, it just to look at you don't have to.
That's exactly what it is, is, is perfectly fine.
OK.
Great.
Yeah.
Good.
Call sack.
Great.
Excellent.
Awesome.
Yeah.

all right, Zach, let me see if I could pull the host back from you because what I'd like to just finish up on.
I mean, I know, um, I wanted to get in front of you with these new features because our contract with Apartment IQ is up in December and I don't want this to scare you at all.
But these are the pricing, that is our standard rate card and that's what brand new customers are seeing, right?
They're coming in and it's two tiers, you're either a premium member, you get concessions, the N er apartment IQ 2.0 we actually didn't even talk about that.
Um That is a new market survey tab.
Uh, that's coming, I don't have any collateral to show you just yet because we're finishing up our closed beta of the market survey tab.
But we are expecting that to launch before the end of this year.
So, right in time for your new contract and that will include things like fees and deposits.
Is there parking?
And is it a charge, is it covered?
What are the pet fees?
What are the application fees?
So it's a more traditional market survey experience.

That part, uh part property managers are probably more used to working with versus someone like you who's in the data and who's looking at the units, you know, just to kind, speak to both of those camps.

Uh, and then enterprise, I don't think you'd be a fit for enterprise here just because I think some of these features with only one or, or few users aren't that helpful for you, like having a single sign on through your Google or your oracle, whatever it is that you guys are accessing your network for the daily data export didn't sound too appealing.
Um But as a legacy user, as one of our partners for a very long time now we're not trying to price anybody out and this is the pricing.
I like to show my customers as the most relevant if you love things just the way they are.
I can understand that.
I mean, I was working for you over the last year.
You can stick with what you're doing.
My understanding is this is the pricing that you're currently on.
So there wouldn't be any real change.
You'd still get the pricing and availability, your unit level reporting and those scheduled reports you'd even get this.
Um That's actually an interesting question.
A nuance that I have to check back on because the new reports have the concessions details in it.
So I think you might be stuck, not stuck, but you'd have the old reports, but that is a new one I'd have to look into.

Um And then the premium, I wouldn't be looking to double your price if something like concessions and n er mattered to you, a lot of customers your size, who've been with us a little while I've been able to kind of get in the 150 range.
Um So kind of meeting us halfway uh to have access to some of those premium features if we can agree to a two year contract, for instance, I can definitely get that.

Um Otherwise there may be a little less room for me if you, if there as another property, I know you mentioned that there's just one, but if you guys have a plan to bring in another property, I can go to the building, uh our billing team and say, hey, they might double in size, which is kind of funny to say because you only have one,
right?

Um So these are kind of the levers that I can pull, but you let me know if it would be helpful for me to go to the billing team and say, hey, Field Stone's been with us the last year concessions and n er looked interesting to them.
Uh I know I can't really show you anything about the market survey tab, but you'd have access to it when it comes out.
Um Contract is up December 30 I think the 21st.

So I'll definitely bring this up.
And so we rolling on that.
I, I don't, first of all, I don't see why we're gonna continue using the product.

All right.
All right, cool.
Anything else on your agenda?
I mean that we didn't get to talk about today?

Zach.

Thanks for your time.
